item.title: DoorDash Poses Disruption Risk to Payments Firms, Analyst Says
item.description: An expected point-of-sale technology rollout from DoorDash Inc. is poised to deal a blow to payments firms such as Toast Inc., according to Rothschild & Co Redburn’s Dominic Ball.

item.title: AI Dictation Startup Wispr in Funding Talks at $2 Billion Value
item.description: Wispr AI Inc., the developer behind the popular voice dictation tool Wispr Flow, is in talks to raise a new round of funding that could more than double its valuation to $2 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Yardeni Stays Calm, Not ‘Freaked Out,’ as Treasury Yields Surge
item.description: Veteran market strategist Ed Yardeni says investors are taking the run-up in Treasury yields in stride and looking through inflation caused by the energy-price spike from the Iran war.

item.title: Copper Rallies Above $14,000 a Ton, Nearing Fresh All-Time High
item.description: Copper jumped above $14,000 a ton, closing in on a record high as a rebound in Chinese demand and rising supply risks outweigh concerns about the Iran war’s impact on global growth.

item.title: KKR-Backed Ambulance Firm GMR Shrinks IPO Target to $479 Million
item.description: GMR Solutions Inc., an air and ground emergency medical services company that counts KKR & Co. among its backers, revised the terms of its IPO and is seeking to raise $479 million, down from an earlier target of as much as $798 million.

item.title: New Beef Records Add Urgency to Trump Moves to Tackle Inflation
item.description: US consumer beef prices surged to new all-time highs, adding urgency to the Trump administration’s efforts to tackle inflation.

item.title: VC Firm Takes Less-Is-More Approach With $450 Million Fund
item.description: Kevin Hartz, a co-founder of Eventbrite and longtime startup investor, has watched as venture capital firms unveil multibillion-dollar funds to back cash-hungry AI companies at every stage of their lifecycles. He’s taking a different approach.A-Star, the early-stage venture firm Hartz launched with former Coatue investor Bennett Siegel, is set to announce on Tuesday that it’s raised $450 million for its third fund — its largest to date, but a far cry from the $15 billion fund that Andreessen Hor

item.title: Iraqi Supertanker Pulls Back From US Hormuz Blockade
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains largely shuttered, with Iran-linked vessels dominating what little traffic is moving across the waterway. An Iraqi supertanker’s rare passage has come to a halt after a retreat from the US naval blockade line.

item.title: Fed’s Goolsbee Says Services Inflation May Point to Overheating
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee said inflation readings show pervasive price pressures in the US economy and may even indicate an overheating.

item.title: Here Are the Key Takeaways for the US CPI Report for April
item.description: Here are the key takeaways on the US consumer price index report for April, released Tuesday:

item.title: Tech Firm CGI Picks Tim Hurlebaus as CEO After AI Fears Crush Share Price
item.description: Canadian IT consulting firm CGI Inc. appointed a new chief executive officer after the company’s share price tumbled about 40% in a year, largely due to concerns about disruption from artificial intelligence upstarts such as Anthropic PBC.

item.title: CME to Create Futures Market for Computing Power Backing AI
item.description: CME Group Inc., the largest US derivatives exchange, and Silicon Data are teaming up to create a futures market for computing power, a key factor needed to help power the AI boom.

item.title: NetEase Veteran Raises $100 Million, Backs Gaming Royalty for ‘Star-Wars’-Led Comeback
item.description: Simon Zhu’s new GreaterThan Group is investing in revered video-game creators, including ‘Mass Effect’ director Casey Hudson

item.title: Deutsche Bank, Nasdaq Back Crypto Firm Elliptic in $120 Million Round
item.description: Blockchain analytics firm Elliptic Enterprises Ltd. has raised $120 million in a new funding round backed by Deutsche Bank AG and Nasdaq Inc.’s venture arm, as large financial institutions expand their digital-asset offerings.

item.title: Treasuries Hold Losses as Rising Gas Costs Fuel Inflation
item.description: Treasuries maintained their losses after a key consumer prices gauge rose more than economists’ estimates, supporting wagers that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates next year to control inflation.

item.title: Rexel CEO Sees More Deals in Wake of Revere Acquistion in US
item.description: French electrical equipment distributor Rexel SA plans to make more bolt-on acquisitions in countries such the US and Canada to tap a boom in data centers and manufacturing, after announcing its 16th deal in North America since 2020.

item.title: Russia’s Oil Flows Slide as Drone, Storm Alerts Disrupt Key Port
item.description: Only one Russian crude cargo was loaded at Novorossiysk on the Black Sea last week

item.title: EDF Said to Plan Sale of Chinese Renewables Amid Nuclear Shift
item.description: Electricite de France SA is working with an adviser to sell its renewable energy assets in China as the French state-owned utility refocuses on European nuclear power projects, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: US Inflation Accelerates as Gas, Rent and Food Prices Climb
item.description: US inflation continued to accelerate in April amid an ongoing climb in gasoline prices driven by the Iran war and a jump in the cost of groceries.

item.title: SAP Invests in AI Automation Startup N8n at $5.2 Billion Value
item.description: SAP SE is investing in workflow automation platform n8n, doubling the startup’s valuation to $5.2 billion, as the German software giant builds out its artificial intelligence offering.

item.title: Brazil Inflation Picks Up Again, Nearing Top of Target Ceiling
item.description: Brazil’s inflation picked up as expected in April, pressured by more expensive food and fuel, to bump up against the top of the central bank’s target range.

item.title: Ramaphosa’s Political Peril Earns Shrug from Investors for Now
item.description: South Africa’s rand is shrugging off the threat that President Cyril Ramaphosa may be impeached, reflecting a bet that economic reforms he has championed will endure if he falls.

item.title: Waymo Recalls Robotaxis After Vehicle Drove on a Flooded Road
item.description: Waymo is recalling thousands of robotaxis to fix a software issue that could cause the autonomous vehicles to drive on flooded roads.

item.title: Stock Futures Drop on Hotter-Than-Forecast Core CPI, Oil Climbs
item.description: Traders pulled back from risk assets with a torrid run in US equities running out of steam as April inflation data came in hotter than economists’ estimates and worsening tensions between Washington and Iran sent oil prices higher.

item.title: Second Qatari LNG Tanker Exits Hormuz After Going Dark
item.description: A second Qatari liquefied natural gas tanker appeared to have exited the Strait of Hormuz, after a shipment successfully transited the chokepoint over the weekend.

item.title: Instructure Reaches Deal With Hackers to Return Canvas User Data
item.description: Instructure Inc. said it reached an agreement with hackers who disrupted universities’ online coursework and testing on the company’s Canvas platform, with the attackers returning stolen data and pledging not to extort the education software maker’s customers.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 12, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.4% as of 7:47 a.m. in New York amid a retreat in high-flying technology stocks.

item.title: AI Mania Makes Old-School Industrials Behave Like Chip Stocks
item.description: Optimism surrounding the potential for industrial companies to profit from the artificial intelligence boom has fueled record-setting momentum in the sector. Now worries are mounting that the group’s link to AI may be getting too tight.

item.title: The US and China Are Saving Oil From a Crisis
item.description: Record American exports and slowing Chinese imports are keeping a lid on prices — for now.

item.title: Analysis of US CPI Report for April
item.description: Pedestrians at a farmers market in Washington. Photographer: Al Drago/Bloomberg
